experiment: M_EXT2 — C. elegans Connectome True External Validation of Functional Proximity Law
description: True external validation of the Functional Proximity Law. Dataset: C. elegans hermaphrodite connectome, 15 major hub neurons, 2 layers. CRITICAL: both the data AND the layer definitions are fully independent of IRDME. Data source: White et al. (1986) electron micrograph reconstruction, as tabulated by Varshney et al. (2011) PLoS Comput Biol. Layer 1 (chemical_synapse): directed neurotransmitter connections (S/Sp type entries). Layer 2 (gap_junction): undirected electrical coupling (EJ type entries). The layer distinction is a biological fundamental defined by the original neuroscience authors, not by IRDME. Law prediction: r(chemical_synapse vs gap_junction) > 0 because both layers encode direct physical neuron-to-neuron coupling (same relational regime: physical contact between neurons), just via different biophysical mechanisms. The major command interneurons (AVAL/AVAR/AVBL/AVBR) are hubs in the C. elegans literature independently — if they are hubs in both layers, the law is confirmed on fully external data.

hypotheses.statement: r(chemical_synapse <-> gap_junction) > 0 — hub neurons in the chemical synapse layer are also hubs in the gap junction layer, because both layers encode direct physical neuron-to-neuron coupling (same relational regime). The command interneurons AVAL and AVAR, which are known top hubs in the C. elegans literature, are predicted to be top hubs in both layers.
AVAL or AVAR is the top hub in the chemical_synapse layer — this is independently confirmed in the C. elegans neuroscience literature (White 1986, Varshney 2011).
